subject: HIST
course_description: Examines history of Central Eurasia as nexus commercial, cultural and political exchange in Eurasia from the Bronze Age to the rise of European Imperialism. Topics include: nomadic empires, oasis merchants, barbarians and empires, Buddhism, Islam, European adventurers, pre-modern globalization.
course_notes: (This course introduces students to the history of the "Silk Road," the central nexus of commercial, cultural and political exchange in Eurasia over the course of nearly three millennia. From the Bronze Age to the rise of European sea-borne hegemony, the Silk Road sent the horse and Buddhism to China, silk and the Black Death to Europe, Islam and sufi mysticism into South Asia. From the great world Empires of Alexander the Great to that of Chingis Khan to the mercantile city-states of Central Asia and Sinkiang, the lands of the Silk Road shaped human history profoundly. The story of the region will be told by ancient mummies and Chinese monks, Persian epic heroes and Arab ambassadors, Venetian merchants and Turkic warrior princes. And in these stories, students will hear the tale of the world's first globalization and see, it is hoped, a mirror of their own world.)
